summary: Recording suffers from out‑of‑phase stereo channels that need inversion to become listenable; no musical details provided.
review: The sole review points out a serious technical flaw: the two stereo channels are 180 degrees out of phase, resulting in a thin or hollow sound until one channel is inverted. Apart from this issue, the reviewer gives the tape a tentative three‑star rating, indicating it might be better with some audio work, but offers no commentary on the performance itself.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one brief review focusing on a major recording defect; no information on musical quality, so rating is driven by technical problems.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo channels are 180° out of phase causing cancellation and muffled sound; inversion of one channel is required for acceptable clarity.
